faqs.question: Is the scan painful?
faqs.answer: No—just cool gel and gentle pressure.

faqs.question: How soon can I resume normal activities?
What clothing should I wear?
faqs.answer: Loose-fitting clothes that can be rolled up above the knee or elbow.

faqs.question: Will insurance cover the test?
faqs.answer: Most insurers reimburse—our front desk assists with cashless or reimbursement forms.

seo.title: Venous Colour Doppler Ultrasound: Safe, Painless Scan
seo.description: Discover how a Venous Colour Doppler Ultrasound Scan at Cadabams Diagnostics detects DVT, varicose veins & more—quick, painless, and radiation-free.

test.markdown:
## Venous Colour Doppler Ultrasound Scan Overview

### What the test evaluates
- Detects \*\*deep-vein thrombosis (DVT)\*\* and superficial clots
- Measures \*\*blood-flow direction and speed\*\*
- Assesses \*\*vein wall compressibility\*\* and \*\*valve function\*\*
### Why Cadabams Diagnostics is trusted
- \*\*25+ years\*\* of imaging excellence
- \*\*NABL-accredited\*\* labs & \*\*ICMR-approved\*\* protocols
- \*\*Same-day soft-copy\*\* reports via secure email
---
## What is Venous Colour Doppler Ultrasound Scan?
### Definition and technology behind colour Doppler
A \*\*venous colour Doppler scan\*\* is a \*\*real-time ultrasound\*\* that adds \*\*colour-coded images\*\* to show blood moving toward or away from the probe. This lets the technologist “see” clots, reflux, or blockages that standard black-and-white ultrasound can miss.
### Difference between standard ultrasound and colour Doppler
| Feature | Standard Ultrasound | Colour Doppler |
|------------------------|---------------------|----------------|
| Shows anatomy | Yes | Yes |
| Shows blood flow | No | Yes (colour) |
| Detects clots | Limited | High accuracy |
---
## Types of Venous Colour Doppler Ultrasound Scan
- \*\*Upper-limb venous Doppler\*\* – arms, subclavian, jugular veins
- \*\*Lower-limb venous Doppler\*\* – thighs, calves, popliteal veins
- \*\*Whole-leg vs targeted segmental studies\*\* – chosen by the radiologist based on symptoms
---

## When and Who Needs to Take a Venous Colour Doppler Ultrasound Scan?

### Symptoms indicating a venous scan
- Sudden \*\*leg pain or heaviness\*\*
- \*\*Swelling\*\* in one limb
- \*\*Warmth or redness\*\* over a vein
- Visible \*\*varicose veins\*\* or \*\*skin ulcers\*\*
### At-risk populations
- \*\*Post-surgery\*\* patients on bed rest
- \*\*Pregnancy\*\* (hormonal changes + pressure on pelvic veins)
- \*\*Sedentary\*\* office workers or long-haul travelers
- Smokers, cancer patients, or those on hormone therapy
---

## Risks & Limitations of the Venous Colour Doppler Ultrasound Scan

### Minimal risk profile
- \*\*No radiation\*\*, \*\*no needles\*\*, \*\*no contrast dye\*\*
- Safe for \*\*pregnant women\*\* and \*\*kidney-compromised patients\*\*
### Limitations
- \*\*Obesity\*\* or \*\*severe edema\*\* can blur images
- Very \*\*small calf veins\*\* may need follow-up MRI or venography
---

## List of Parameters Considered During the Venous Colour Doppler Ultrasound Scan

- \*\*Vein compressibility\*\* – healthy veins collapse under probe pressure
- \*\*Blood flow direction & velocity\*\* – checks for reflux or obstruction
- \*\*Presence of thrombus or reflux\*\* – clot seen as a filling defect
- \*\*Valve competency\*\* – valves should prevent backward flow
---

## Reasons for Taking a Venous Colour Doppler Ultrasound Scan

- \*\*Rule out DVT\*\* before long flights or surgery
- \*\*Assess chronic venous insufficiency\*\* causing leg ulcers
- \*\*Pre-operative mapping\*\* for varicose-vein surgery
---

## Benefits of Taking the Venous Colour Doppler Ultrasound Scan

- \*\*Non-invasive and painless\*\* – just gel and gentle probe pressure
- \*\*Real-time results\*\* – see blood flow instantly on screen
- \*\*Zero radiation\*\* – repeat as often as needed
---

## Illnesses Diagnosed with Venous Colour Doppler Ultrasound Scan

- \*\*Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT)\*\*
- \*\*Varicose veins & venous reflux\*\*
- \*\*Superficial thrombophlebitis\*\*
- \*\*Venous malformations or arteriovenous fistulas\*\*
---

## Preparing for Venous Colour Doppler Ultrasound Scan

- Wear \*\*loose clothing\*\*; remove jewellery from limbs
- \*\*Fasting is not required\*\* – eat and drink normally
- Bring \*\*doctor’s referral\*\* and any \*\*previous scan reports\*\*
---
## Pre-requisites Venous Colour Doppler Ultrasound Scan
- \*\*Doctor’s prescription\*\* (as per insurer or hospital protocol)
- \*\*Clean, dry skin\*\* on the limb to be scanned—avoid lotions
---
## Best Time to Take the Venous Colour Doppler Ultrasound Scan
- \*\*Morning slots\*\* reduce leg swelling from day-long standing
- \*\*Urgent booking within 24 h\*\* if acute DVT is suspected
---
## Eligibility
- \*\*Adults of all ages\*\*
- \*\*Safe in pregnancy\*\* – no radiation risk
- \*\*Pediatric scans\*\* on physician request (smaller probe used)
---
## Procedure for Taking a Venous Colour Doppler Ultrasound Scan
1. \*\*Duration\*\*: 30–45 minutes
2. \*\*Position\*\*: Lie on your back; the limb may be slightly rotated
3. \*\*Gel application\*\*: Water-based gel helps sound waves travel
4. \*\*Probe movement\*\*: Technologist glides the probe along veins; you may hear “whooshing” sounds representing blood flow
---
## Caution Before Taking the Test
- Inform staff of any \*\*contrast allergies\*\* (rarely used)
- Remove \*\*compression stockings 2 h before\*\* the exam to avoid false-negative results
---
